title: inula verbascifolia subsp. aschersoniana
intro: Inula verbascifolia subsp. aschersoniana, also known as the Ascherson's golden samphire, is a subspecies of the Inula verbascifolia plant, a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Inula verbascifolia subsp. aschersoniana is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
